---
name: Benchmark contribution
about: Submit a bench.sh report for your phone
title: 'bench: add <device> results'
labels: enhancement, benchmarks
---

## Device

- **Manufacturer (ro.product.manufacturer):**
- **Model (ro.product.model):**
- **SoC (ro.board.platform or ro.soc.model):**
- **RAM (MiB from /proc/meminfo):**
- **Android version (ro.build.version.release):**
- **Ollama version:**
- **Model benchmarked (e.g. qwen2.5:1.5b):**

## New file

- [ ] `benchmarks/<device-slug>.md` — committed exactly as generated by `bench.sh`, with the `<!-- Verified on: ... -->` header intact.

## Testing conditions

Things that change the numbers and are worth noting:

- [ ] Phone **plugged in** to power (not on battery)
- [ ] Phone **cool** at start of run (not thermally throttling)
- [ ] Ran `bench.sh --runs 3` (or higher) for a stable warm median
- [ ] **No background apps** (airplane mode is fine; kill Chrome/YouTube/etc.)
- [ ] Ollama server was **freshly started** (not left running from a previous session)
- [ ] The **screen was on** at full brightness (affects SoC governor on some devices)

Note anything else that seemed to affect the numbers (outside temp, specific
ROM, battery saver mode, etc.).

## Prompts version

What version of `prompts.json` did you benchmark against? The file header
shows this (`"version": N`). If you changed the prompts, don't submit — the
whole point is cross-device comparability against a fixed set.

- [ ] Ran against `prompts.json` version **1** (current)
- [ ] My prompts.json matches the repo's — I did not modify it.

## Warm median vs range

From the Summary section of your report:

- **Warm median:** ___ tok/s
- **Warm range:** ___ .. ___ tok/s
- **Cold first call:** ___ tok/s

If the range is more than ~20% wide, run with more `--runs` — unstable
numbers suggest thermal or background-process noise.

## Anything else

Notes for future contributors, caveats, things you learned about your
phone's thermal behaviour, etc.
